topic.name: Cricket Players and History
topic.description: This cluster covers notable cricketers from various eras, their achievements, and contributions to the sport of cricket.

Document 1: Norman Yardley was an English cricketer who played for Cambridge University, Yorkshire County Cricket Club and England.
He captained Yorkshire from 1948 to 1955 and England on fourteen occasions between 1947 and 1950.
Document 2: Leah Poulton is a female Australian cricketer who plays for New South Wales and Australia.
She is a specialist batsman who usually opens the batting.
Document 3: Norm O'Neill was a cricketer who played for New South Wales and Australia.
He was one of the foremost batsmen in the Australian team, scoring three Test centuries.
Document 4: Arnold Fothergill was an English professional cricketer who played first-class cricket for Somerset and the Marylebone Cricket Club.
He appeared for England in two Test matches in 1889.
Document 5: The match between Port Phillip and Van Diemen's Land in 1851 is recognized as the first first-class cricket match.
It was held to celebrate the separation of Victoria from New South Wales.
Document 6: Joe Darling was an Australian cricketer who played 34 Test matches as a specialist batsman between 1894 and 1905.
He was captain of the Australian cricket team in England in 1902.
Document 7: Jos Buttler is an English cricketer who plays for Lancashire County Cricket Club and England.
He is the current Vice-Captain of the England ODI team.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling Pay-Per-View Events
topic.description: The documents discuss various professional wrestling pay-per-view events produced by Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA) and World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE), highlighting their matches, outcomes, and notable occurrences.

Document 1: Genesis (2005) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA) promotion.
The main event was a Six Man Tag Team match pitting the team of Rhino and Team 3D against the team of Jeff Jarrett and America's Most Wanted.
Document 2: Sacrifice (2008) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by the Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA) promotion.
The main event was a Three Way match for the TNA World Heavyweight Championship between then-champion Samoa Joe and challengers Kaz and Scott Steiner.
Document 3: WrestleMania XXIV was the twenty-fourth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
Nine professional wrestling matches were scheduled for the event, which featured a supercard, a scheduling of more than one main bout.
Document 4: Turning Point (2007) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by the Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA) promotion.
The main event was a Six Man Tag Team match, in which the team of Samoa Joe, Kevin Nash, and Eric Young defeated The Angle Alliance.
Document 5: Unbreakable was a professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by the Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA) promotion.
The main event at Unbreakable was a Three Way match for the TNA X Division Championship.

topic.name: Literary Contributions and Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses the contributions of various authors and educators to literature and education across different cultures and periods.

Document 1: Percy Shaw Jeffrey was a respected English schoolmaster and author of several books on a range of topics, including significant contributions towards the teaching of phonetics in schools.
Jeffrey taught at a variety of schools before spending sixteen years as headmaster at Colchester Royal Grammar School.

He wrote over fifty works of fiction, contributing significantly to the genre of thrillers.

topic.description: This topic encompasses significant figures in various sports, highlighting their achievements and contributions to their respective fields.

He received a two-year athletics ban in 2003 after testing positive for a banned performance-enhancing drug.
Document 2: Charles Barkley is an American retired professional basketball player and current analyst on television.
He was voted the league's Most Valuable Player and won two gold medals in the Olympics.
Document 3: Howard Staunton was an English chess master regarded as the world's strongest player from 1843 to 1851.
He promoted a chess set of standardised shape that is still used in competitions.
Document 4: Bobby Fischer was an American chess grandmaster and the eleventh World Chess Champion.
He is often considered the greatest chess player of all time.
Document 5: Paavo Nurmi was a Finnish middle- and long-distance runner known as the 'Flying Finn.'
He set 22 official world records and won nine gold medals in the Olympics.
Document 7: Milos Raonic is a Canadian professional tennis player who reached a career-high world No. 4 ranking.
He has been ranked continuously inside the top 20 since August 2012.

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: The topic covers the historical annual rowing competition between the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge, known as The Boat Race, detailing its significant events and outcomes over the years.

Document 1: The 13th Boat Race took place on the River Thames on 15 March 1856.
The race was first held in 1829.
Document 2: The 41st Boat Race took place on 7 April 1884.
The race takes place on the 4.2 miles (6.8 km) Championship Course on the River Thames.
Document 3: The 67th Boat Race took place on 23 March 1910.
The rivalry is a major point of honour between the two universities.
Document 4: The 50th Boat Race took place on 22 March 1893.
Oxford went into the event as reigning champions.
Document 5: The 35th Boat Race took place on 13 April 1878.
First held in 1829, the race takes place on the 4.2 miles (6.8 km) Championship Course.
Document 6: The 126th Boat Race took place on 5 April 1980.
It was won by Oxford in the closest finish for a century.
Document 7: The 89th Boat Race took place on 24 March 1937.
The victory took the overall record in the event to 47 – 41 in Cambridge's favour.

topic.name: State Highways in the US
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways in the United States, detailing their routes, history, and characteristics.

Document 1: SR 319 is a very short, two-lane state highway in the western portion of the U.S. state of Ohio.
SR 319 was established in the early 1930s.
Document 2: M-247 is a north-south state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan.
The highway has been part of the state trunkline highway system since the 1920s.
Document 3: M-77 is a state trunkline highway in the Upper Peninsula of the US state of Michigan.
Originally designated by 1919, the highway has not been changed much in its history.
Document 4: State Highway 168 is a short state highway located entirely within the city of Galveston in the U.S. state of Texas.
The route was first proposed in 1933.
Document 5: M-84 is a state trunkline highway in the Lower Peninsula of the US state of Michigan.
The northern terminus is at a pair of intersections with M-25 on the east side of the Saginaw River in Bay City.
Document 6: M-41 was the designation of a former state trunkline highway in the Lower Peninsula of the US state of Michigan.
The highway was created by 1919 and lasted until 1926.
Document 7: State Route 27 is a 90-mile-long state highway serving Whitman and Spokane counties.
The highway has been part of the state highway system since 1913.

topic.name: Naval Warfare History
topic.description: The documents discuss various classes of battleships and aircraft carriers, focusing on their designs, historical significance, and roles in naval warfare during major conflicts such as World War I and World War II.

Document 1: Bismarck was laid down in July 1936 and completed in September 1940
Bismarck conducted only one operation, Operation Rheinübung, a sortie into the North Atlantic to raid supply convoys
Document 2: The Petropavlovsk class was a class of three pre-dreadnought battleships built for the Imperial Russian Navy during the 1890s
All three ships participated in the Battle of Port Arthur on the second day of the war
Document 3: The two Hiyō-class aircraft carriers were built for the Imperial Japanese Navy during World War II
Both ships participated in several battles during the Guadalcanal Campaign in late 1942
Document 4: The German aircraft carrier Graf Zeppelin was the lead ship in a class of two carriers of the same name ordered by the Kriegsmarine
She was the only aircraft carrier launched by Germany and represented part of the Kriegsmarine's attempt to create a well-balanced oceangoing fleet
Document 5: The history of the Royal Australian Navy traces the development of the Royal Australian Navy from the colonisation of Australia by the British in 1788
During the course of World War II, the RAN operated more than 350 fighting and support vessels
Document 6: The Lavochkin La-7 was a piston-engined Soviet fighter developed during World War II
It was a development and refinement of the Lavochkin La-5
Document 7: HMS Dreadnought was a battleship of the Royal Navy that revolutionised naval power
Her entry into service in 1906 represented such a paradigm shift in naval technology that her name came to be associated with an entire generation of battleships

topic.name: Political Figures and Their Impact
topic.description: This topic encompasses the lives and contributions of various political figures throughout history, highlighting their roles, ideologies, and significant events they were involved in.

1: Mauro De Mauro was an Italian investigative journalist.
His disappearance remains one of the unsolved mysteries in Italian history.
2: Johnston de Peyster was a soldier in the Union Army during the American Civil War.
He is best remembered for raising the first Union flag over the Confederate capitol of Richmond, Virginia.
3: Ron Paul ran on a platform that included non-interventionism in foreign conflicts and decriminalization of illegal drugs.
He received very little media coverage during his campaign.
4: Rogers Morton served as the U.S. Secretary of the Interior and Secretary of Commerce.
He established an environmental record while serving in the House of Representatives.
5: Thomas Hinde was Northern Kentucky's first physician and a member of the British Royal Navy.
He played a critical role in the American Revolutionary War through his vaccinations against smallpox.
6: Blas Ople was a Filipino journalist and politician who held several high-ranking positions in the Philippine government.
He was a vocal supporter for allowing a limited United States military presence in the Philippines.
7: William Burnet was a British civil servant and colonial administrator who served as governor of New York and New Jersey.
His tenure in New Jersey was without major controversies, although he set a precedent for accepting bribes.

topic.name: Biodiversity and Animal Behavior
topic.description: This cluster discusses various species of animals, their habitats, behaviors, and ecological roles.

Document 1: The wood turtle is a turtle endemic to North America.
It spends a great deal of time in or near the water of wide rivers.
Document 2: Aerodramus is a genus of small, dark, cave-nesting birds.
What distinguishes Aerodramus swiftlets from other swifts is their ability to use a simple but effective form of echolocation.
Document 3: Ypresiomyrma is an extinct genus of ants in the subfamily Myrmeciinae.
The behaviour of these ants would have been similar to that of extant Myrmeciinae ants.
Document 4: Deimatic behaviour in animals means any pattern of threatening or startling behaviour.
Displays are classified as deimatic or aposematic by the responses of the animals that see them.
Document 5: Portia fimbriata is a jumping spider found in Australia and Southeast Asia.
The hunting tactics of Portia are versatile and adaptable.
Document 6: The Kerry slug is a rare species of medium-sized to large air-breathing land slug.
The species appears to require environments that have high humidity and acidic soil.
Document 7: The willow ptarmigan is a bird in the grouse subfamily Tetraoninae.
Nesting takes place in the spring when clutches of four to ten eggs are laid in a scrape on the ground.

topic.name: Biodiversity and Conservation

topic.name: Early 20th Century Naval Warfare
topic.description: This topic encompasses the development, deployment, and operational history of naval ships and strategies during the early 20th century, particularly focusing on the Imperial German and Japanese navies.

Document 1: SMS Rostock was a light cruiser of the Karlsruhe class built by the German Kaiserliche Marine.

Document 2: Nisshin was a Kasuga-class armored cruiser of the Imperial Japanese Navy.

Document 3: SMS Schwaben was the fourth ship of the Wittelsbach class of pre-dreadnought battleships of the German Imperial Navy.

Document 4: SMS Weissenburg was one of the first ocean-going battleships of the Imperial German Navy.
The Brandenburg-class battleships were unique for their era in that they carried six large-caliber guns in three twin turrets.
Document 6: SMS Bremse was a Brummer-class minelaying light cruiser of the German Kaiserliche Marine.

Document 7: Z11 Bernd von Arnim was a Type 1934A-class destroyer built for Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine in the late 1930s.

Document 2: The Avro Vulcan is a jet-powered tailless delta wing high-altitude strategic bomber, which was operated by the Royal Air Force (RAF) from 1956 until 1984.
The Vulcan was the backbone of the United Kingdom's airborne nuclear deterrent during much of the Cold War.
Document 6: The Petlyakov Pe-8 was a Soviet heavy bomber designed before World War II, and the only four-engine bomber the USSR built during the war.
It was used to bomb Berlin in August 1941.

topic.name: Major League Baseball Players
topic.description: This topic encompasses the careers and achievements of various professional baseball players in Major League Baseball (MLB).

Document 1: Ryan Braun is an American baseball left fielder for the Milwaukee Brewers of Major League Baseball (MLB).
Braun has won the National League (NL) Most Valuable Player (MVP) in 2011.
Document 2: Aubrey Huff is an American former professional baseball player who played in Major League Baseball (MLB).
Huff batted left-handed but threw right-handed.
Document 3: Jeriome Robertson was an American left-handed baseball pitcher who played three seasons in Major League Baseball for the Houston Astros and Cleveland Indians.
Robertson posted a win-loss record of 16-12 with a 5.71 earned run average.
Document 4: Oyster Burns was a professional baseball player whose career spanned 15 seasons in Major League Baseball (MLB).
Burns compiled a career batting average of .300 with 870 runs scored.
Document 5: Ryan Hanigan is an American professional baseball catcher for the Boston Red Sox of Major League Baseball (MLB).
Hanigan recorded his first major league hit in that at-bat.
Document 6: Joey Hamilton is a former Major League Baseball pitcher who played professionally for nine seasons.
Hamilton recorded a 74-73 win-loss record and a 4.44 earned run average.
Document 7: Sam Fuld is an American professional baseball outfielder for the Oakland Athletics of Major League Baseball.
Fuld was drafted by the Chicago Cubs in 2004.

topic.name: Norwegian and British Railways
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history, operation, and infrastructure of railway systems in Norway and the UK.

1: The Numedal Line is a 92.8-kilometer long railway line that runs up the Numedal valley between Kongsberg and Rødberg in Buskerud, Norway.
The first plans for a line through Numedal were launched after the Sørland Line reached in Kongsberg in 1871.
2: The Jæren Commuter Rail is a commuter train service operated along the westernmost part of the Sørland Line in Jæren, Norway.
The service acts as a commuter rail connecting Stavanger to its suburbs, including Sandnes.

topic.name: Medieval Eastern European Monarchs
topic.description: This topic encompasses the reigns and significant events involving various monarchs in Eastern Europe during the medieval period, particularly focusing on their interactions with the Byzantine and Ottoman Empires.

Document 1: Louis I of Hungary was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1342 and King of Poland from 1370.
He inherited a centralized kingdom and a rich treasury from his father.
Document 2: Michael I Komnenos Doukas was the founder and first ruler of the Despotate of Epirus.
He established himself as ruler in Epirus, becoming a centre of resistance against the Latin Crusaders.
Document 3: Skanderbeg was the ruler of Albania who had been leading a rebellion against the Ottoman Empire since 1443.
He allied himself with several Western European monarchs to consolidate his domains.
Document 4: The Byzantine – Ottoman Wars were a series of decisive conflicts between the Ottoman Turks and Byzantines.
These wars led to the final destruction of the Byzantine Empire.
Document 5: The Muslim conquest of Sicily began in June 827 and lasted until 902, impacting Byzantine control.
The conquest was initiated by the Aghlabid emirs of Ifriqiya.
Document 6: The Battle of Halmyros was fought between the forces of the Frankish Duchy of Athens and the mercenaries of the Catalan Company.
The battle resulted in a devastating victory for the Catalans.
Document 7: John Sigismund Zápolya was King of Hungary and the first Prince of Transylvania.
He was involved in the political struggles between the Ottoman Sultan Suleiman and Ferdinand I.

topic.name: Australian Military History
topic.description: The documents discuss notable Australian military figures and units during World War II, highlighting their contributions and experiences.

Document 1: Virgil Brennan was an Australian aviator and flying ace of the Second World War.
Brennan was officially credited with the destruction of 10 Axis aircraft.
Document 2: Peter Isaacson is an Australian publisher and decorated military pilot.
He served in the Royal Australian Air Force as a pilot with RAF Bomber Command during World War II.
Document 3: The Long Range Desert Group was a reconnaissance and raiding unit of the British Army during the Second World War.
The LRDG was formed specifically to carry out deep penetration, covert reconnaissance patrols.
Document 4: The 1st Special Air Service Brigade was a fictional brigade during the Second World War.
It was used to mislead Axis commanders about the strength of Allied forces in North Africa.
Document 6: The 31st / 51st Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army, which served during World War II.
The battalion undertook garrison duties in Dutch New Guinea in 1943 – 44.

topic.name: Music Albums and Songs
topic.description: The documents discuss various music albums and songs, highlighting their themes, production, and artistic influences.

Document 1: 'Zoo Station' is a song by the rock band U2.
The song's lyrics were inspired by a surrealistic story about Berlin from World War II.
Document 2: Commit This to Memory is the second studio album by American rock band Motion City Soundtrack.
The album became the band 's breakthrough, with lead single 'Everything Is Alright' becoming the band 's signature song.
Document 3: Documentaly is the fifth studio album by Japanese band Sakanaction, released on September 28, 2011.
The band decided to release three singles prior to the album, as a way to alleviate the pressure.
Document 4: Good Kid, M.A.A.D City is the second studio album by American rapper Kendrick Lamar.
The album serves as Lamar's major label debut, after his signing to Aftermath and Interscope.
Document 5: 'Love Is Blindness' is a song by the rock band U2, and the twelfth and final track on their 1991 album Achtung Baby.
Thematically, the song describes a failing romance, mixing personal themes with imagery of metaphorical acts of terrorism.
Document 6: So Amazin' is the third studio album by American singer Christina Milian.
The album's musical style is primarily urban and R & B, in contrast to the pop sounds of Milian's previous albums.
Document 7: 'Boogie Chillen' is a blues song first recorded by John Lee Hooker in 1948.
Hooker's guitar work on the song inspired several well-known guitarists to take up the instrument.

topic.name: Popular Love Songs
topic.description: The documents discuss various popular love songs by different artists, highlighting their themes, musical styles, and commercial success.

Document 1: 'Marry You' is a pop song featuring strong influences of doo-wop.
the recording focuses on spontaneous marriage and therefore, since its release, has frequently been used as a proposal song.
Document 2: 'Boyfriend' is a R & B influenced song that features pop beats reminiscent of American record producer Pharrell Williams.
The song was a commercial success, debuting at number two on the Billboard Hot 100.
Document 3: 'Oh Love' is a song by American punk rock band Green Day.
Critical response to the song was mixed; it was praised for its musical style and tone.
Document 4: 'The Way You Love Me' is a song recorded by American R & B singer-songwriter Keri Hilson.
The song is about being in a committed relationship, but the subject's sexual desire not holding back.
Document 5: 'Beautiful' is a pop ballad that discusses inner beauty, as well as self-esteem and insecurity issues.
It has been widely embraced as an anthem by the LGBT community for its message of self-empowerment.
Document 6: 'Numb' is a dance-pop and electropop song with R & B elements.
Lyrically, it is 'an inspirational song about finding one’s destiny, and becoming numb to the negativity.'
Document 7: 'Yeah!' incorporates crunk and R & B in the song's production.
The song topped the US Billboard Hot 100 chart for twelve consecutive weeks.

topic.name: Cultural Contributions and Influences
topic.description: This topic encompasses the significant cultural, political, and artistic contributions of historical figures from various backgrounds.

Document 1: Sakakibara Kenkichi was instrumental in preserving traditional Japanese sword techniques in the early Meiji Era.
his work during this period laid the foundations for the modern sport of kendo.
Document 2: Broder Knudtzon was a Norwegian merchant, politician and benefactor.
he bequeathed his entire library and several artworks to the Royal Norwegian Society of Sciences and Letters.
Document 4: Peter Aquino Aduja was the first Filipino American elected to public office in the United States.
he worked as a teacher, before becoming one of the first Filipino lawyers in Hawaii.
Document 6: Decorating of the Bride is an oil painting by the Serbian artist Paja Jovanović.
the painting is set in an impoverished 19th-century ethnic Albanian household.
Document 7: The Cats of Ulthar is a short story written by American fantasy author H. P. Lovecraft.
the tale relates the story of how a law forbidding the killing of cats came to be in a town called Ulthar.

topic.name: Military Engagements in History
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant battles and military leaders throughout various historical conflicts, particularly focusing on the American Revolutionary War and the Napoleonic Wars.

Document 6: Isaac Davis was a militia officer who commanded a company of Minutemen during the first battle of the American Revolutionary War.
He was among the first killed and was the first American officer to die in the Revolution.
Document 7: The Battle of the Assunpink Creek was a battle between American and British troops during the American Revolutionary War.
It resulted in an American victory following a surprise victory at the Battle of Trenton.

topic.name: NASCAR and Formula One Races
topic.description: The documents discuss notable stock car and Formula One races, detailing race results, key drivers, and significant events during the races.

Document 1: 2008 AMP Energy 500 was the 30th stock car race of the 2008 NASCAR Sprint Cup Series
The race was won by Tony Stewart of the Joe Gibbs Racing team after he started from 34th position.
Document 2: 2009 Dickies 500 was the 34th stock car race of the 2009 NASCAR Sprint Cup Series
The race was won by Kurt Busch of the Penske Racing team after he started from third position.
Document 3: The 1997 European Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 26 October 1997
The race was won by Mika Häkkinen in a McLaren, his first Formula One race victory.
Document 4: The 2015 Mexican Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held at the Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
Nico Rosberg won the race for Mercedes.
Document 5: The 2010 Price Chopper 400 was a NASCAR Sprint Cup Series stock car race
Greg Biffle, of the Roush Fenway Racing team, won the race.
Document 6: The 2000 Japanese Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 8 October 2000
It was the 16th and penultimate round of the 2000 Formula One season.
Document 7: The 2010 Auto Club 500 was a NASCAR Sprint Cup Series race held on February 21, 2010
The race was won by Jimmie Johnson for Hendrick Motorsports.

topic.name: Legal and Social Issues
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legal and social challenges related to individual rights, governance, and societal norms.

Document 1: Kim Davis gained international attention in August 2015 when she defied a U.S. federal court order to issue marriage licenses to same-sex couples.
Her defiance led to her jail sentence, while both supporters and detractors hotly debated her stance in the national media.
Document 2: The Vicinage Clause is a provision in the Sixth Amendment to the United States Constitution regulating the vicinity from which a jury pool may be selected.
The clause limits the vicinity of criminal jury selection to both the state and the federal judicial district where the crime has been committed.
Document 3: Prostitution of children is a form of commercial sexual exploitation of children.
Research suggests that there may be as many as 10 million children involved in prostitution worldwide.
Document 4: United States v. More was a United States Supreme Court case in which the Court held that it had no jurisdiction to hear appeals from criminal cases in the circuit courts by writs of error.
More ensured that the Court's criminal jurisprudence would be limited to writs of error from the state courts.
Document 5: The Independent Women’s Forum is a politically conservative American non-profit organization focused on policy issues of concern to women.
The IWF was founded by activist Rosalie Silberman to promote a 'conservative alternative to feminist tenets.'
Document 6: Article 14 of the Constitution of the Republic of Singapore guarantees to Singapore citizens the rights to freedom of speech and expression, peaceful assembly without arms, and association.
The enjoyment of these rights may be restricted by laws imposed by the Parliament of Singapore.
Document 7: In the United States, compliance requirements are a series of directives United States federal government agencies established that summarize hundreds of federal laws and regulations.
The OMB created fourteen basic and standard compliance requirements that recipients must comply with when receiving and using such federal assistance.

topic.name: James Bond Films and Related Media
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the James Bond film series, its adaptations, and related cinematic works.

Document 1: twelfth spy film in the James Bond series
the fifth to star Roger Moore as the fictional MI6 agent James Bond
Document 2: sixteenth entry in the James Bond film series
first one not to use the title of an Ian Fleming story
Document 3: first installment in the Star Wars prequel trilogy
epic space opera film written and directed by George Lucas
Document 4: first season of the Canadian science fiction – fantasy television series
follows the actions of a secret organization known as the Sanctuary
Document 5: 1998 American animated musical action-comedy-drama film produced by Walt Disney Feature Animation
based on the Chinese legend of Hua Mulan
Document 6: American television serial drama created by Paul Scheuring
the series revolves around two brothers
Document 7: American-British high fantasy film directed by Ron Howard
produced and with a story by George Lucas

topic.name: Historic Architecture and Sites
topic.description: The documents discuss various historic buildings and sites, detailing their architectural significance and historical context.

Document 1: historic meeting hall at 47 Mulberry Street in Pawtucket, Rhode Island
listed on the historic register in 1983
Document 2: St. George's Church is an Anglican church in Trotton
recorded in the National Heritage List for England as a designated Grade I listed building
Document 4: Our Lady Star of the Sea and St Winefride, Amlwch is a Roman Catholic church
is a Grade II * listed building

built on a wealthy former Anglo-Saxon estate by Bishop Odo of Bayeux

became an object of pagan veneration

topic.name: State Highways Overview
topic.description: This topic encompasses the characteristics, history, and significance of various state highways in the United States and Canada.

Document 1: Maryland Route 24 is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The state highway runs 25.17 miles from an entrance to Aberdeen Proving Ground in Edgewood north to the Pennsylvania state line.
Document 2: Tonkin Highway is a 44-kilometre-long highway in Perth, Western Australia.
Planning for the route began in the 1950s, but the first segment was not opened until 1980.
Document 3: U.S. Highway 59 heads along the eastern portion of the state of Oklahoma.
US-59 was first designated in Oklahoma around 1935.
Document 4: King's Highway 33 is a provincially maintained highway in Ontario.
Highway 33 is divided into two sections by the Bay of Quinte.
Document 5: North Carolina Highway 98 is a 44-mile-long state highway.
NC 98 first appeared in 1930 as a state route connecting NC 91 and NC 102.
Document 6: U.S. Route 412 is a U.S. highway in the south-central portion of the United States.
US-412 has two tolled sections in Oklahoma.
Document 7: M-3 is a north-south state trunkline highway in the Detroit metropolitan area.
The trunkline starts in Downtown Detroit and runs through the city in a northeasterly direction.

topic.name: Mathematics and Language
topic.description: The documents cover various fundamental concepts in mathematics and language, including calculus, matrix theory, graph theory, and the Czech language.

Document 1: Period 1 element is one of the chemical elements in the first row of the periodic table.
elements with similar behaviour fall into the same vertical columns.
Document 2: Czech is a West Slavic language strongly influenced by Latin and German.
It is spoken by over 10 million people and is the official language of the Czech Republic.
Document 3: The derivative of a function of a real variable measures the sensitivity to change.
Derivatives are a fundamental tool of calculus.
Document 4: A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers, symbols, or expressions.
A major application of matrices is to represent linear transformations.
Document 5: A directed acyclic graph (DAG) is a finite directed graph with no directed cycles.
DAGs may be used to model many different kinds of information.
Document 6: The topology of an electronic circuit is the form taken by the network of interconnections.
Electronic network topology is related to mathematical topology.
Document 7: A vector space is a collection of objects called vectors.
Vectors in vector spaces do not necessarily have to be arrow-like objects.

topic.name: Cultural and Historical Significance
topic.description: The documents explore various aspects of cultural and historical significance in American society, including music, architecture, activism, and social movements.

1: The song speaks of the labor and environmental problems that the city was facing in 1941
the song's mentions of pollution in Pittsburgh have been sometimes been replaced with verses extolling the city.
2: The Ashland Mill Bridge was built following the Ashland dam break of February 1886
the bridge was added to the state of Connecticut historic register and it was later added to the National Register of Historic Places.
3: Sister Margaret McKenna, M.M.S. is an American Medical Mission Sister and anti-militarist activist
McKenna has been arrested for several acts of civil disobedience since becoming an activist.
4: Larkin is the author of Suburban Youth in Cultural Crisis
They co-authored a book together on social movements.
5: The Joseph Carpenter Silversmith Shop is a historic building
the shop was added to the National Register of Historic Places on October 6, 1970.
6: Potter was an English taxidermist noted for his anthropomorphic dioramas
The exhibition was a well-known and popular example of 'Victorian whimsy' for many years.
7: The Psychology of The Simpsons is a non-fiction book analyzing psychology themes in the television series The Simpsons
The book is meant to appeal both to fans of The Simpsons, and academic students of psychology.

topic.name: Historical Naval Warships
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various classes of historical naval warships, detailing their designs, construction, service histories, and eventual fates.

Document 1: Defence-class ironclads were a class of two warships built for the Royal Navy between 1859 and 1862
They were designed as armoured frigates in response to an invasion scare
Document 2: Kaganovich was a Project 26bis2 Kirov-class cruiser of the Soviet Navy that was built during World War II
She saw no action during the war and served into the Cold War
Document 3: Strelets was an Uragan-class monitor built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the mid-1860s
She was struck from the Navy List in 1900, converted into a floating workshop
Document 4: Amiral Charner was an armored cruiser built for the French Navy in the 1890s
The ship was sunk in early 1916 by a German submarine
Document 5: HMS Illustrious was a Majestic-class pre-dreadnought battleship
She was decommissioned in 1919 and scrapped the following year
Document 6: The Edgar Quinet class was the last type of armored cruiser built for the French Navy
They participated in the blockade of the Adriatic to keep the Austro-Hungarian Navy contained
Document 7: HMS Agincourt was a Minotaur-class armoured frigate built for the Royal Navy during the 1860s
She was placed in reserve two years later and served as a training ship from 1893 to 1909

topic.name: Fungal Taxonomy and Characteristics
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of fungi, their taxonomy, physical characteristics, and ecological roles.

Document 7: Tricholoma vaccinum is a fungus of the agaric genus Tricholoma.
It produces medium-sized fruit bodies that have a distinctive hairy reddish-brown cap.

topic.name: Cultural and Technological Developments
topic.description: The documents explore various advancements and cultural milestones in healthcare, aviation, journalism, music, retail, amusement, and science fiction.

Document 1: Forth Valley Royal Hospital is operated by NHS Forth Valley.
It has Scotland's first fully robotic pharmacy.
Document 2: The Avro Canada CF-103 was a proposed Canadian interceptor.
The aircraft never progressed beyond the mock-up stage.
Document 3: Barbree is the only journalist to have covered every manned space mission in the United States.
He has been present for all 135 space shuttle launches.
Document 4: Voices of the Lifestream is an unofficial tribute album released by OverClocked ReMix.
The album was released to coincide with the 10th anniversary of Final Fantasy VII.
Document 5: Ko Olina Station and Ko Olina Center make up a lifestyle center in the resort town of Ko Olina.
The centers contain a total of approximately 31 retail tenants.
Document 6: Griffon is a steel Dive Coaster roller coaster located at the Busch Gardens Williamsburg amusement park.
The roller coaster features two Immelmann loops and a splashdown.
Document 7: Space Stories was a pulp magazine which published five issues from October 1952 to June 1953.
It was one of the last of these pulp magazines.

topic.name: Video Game Music and Storytelling
topic.description: The documents discuss various video games, focusing on their narratives and musical compositions.

1: Tales of Hearts is a Japanese role-playing game released for the Nintendo DS in Japan and the PlayStation Vita worldwide.
The game follows main protagonist Kor Meteor after he discovers a woman named Kohaku Hearts.
2: Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time is a third-person action-adventure puzzle-platform video game developed and published by Ubisoft.
The gameplay revolves around the Prince's platforming abilities, broken up by fights with the creatures created by the Sands.
3: The music for the 2013 action role-playing game Lightning Returns: Final Fantasy XIII was composed by Masashi Hamauzu.
The music has received a mostly positive response from reviewers.
4: The Mother series is a role-playing video game series created by Shigesato Itoi for Nintendo.
The music of the Mother series includes the soundtracks to all three games.
5: Tales of Vesperia is a Japanese role-playing video game developed by Namco Tales Studio for the Xbox 360 and PlayStation 3.
The game's story theme is justice, and its Characteristic Genre Name is 'RPG to Enforce Justice.'
6: Katamari Damacy is a third-person puzzle-action video game that was published and developed by Namco.
The music of the Katamari Damacy series refers to the soundtracks to all of these games.
7: Dragon Age: Origins is a role-playing video game developed by BioWare and published by Electronic Arts.
The game is played from a third-person perspective that can be shifted to top-down perspective.

topic.name: Maya Archaeology and Ancient Civilizations
topic.description: The documents discuss significant archaeological sites and historical contexts of ancient civilizations, particularly focusing on the Maya civilization.

1: Calakmul is a Maya archaeological site in the Mexican state of Campeche.
Calakmul was one of the largest and most powerful ancient cities ever uncovered in the Maya lowlands.
2: Calakmul is a Maya archaeological site in the Mexican state of Campeche.
Calakmul was a major Maya power within the northern Petén Basin region of the Yucatán Peninsula of southern Mexico.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclone Events
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclones, their formation, impact, and meteorological history.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Gilda in 1973 was the first documented tropical cyclone on record to transition into a subtropical cyclone.
The storm first brought heavy rainfall to Jamaica, causing six deaths and some damage from mudslides.
Document 2: Hurricane Kenneth was the strongest and longest-tracked hurricane of the 2005 Pacific hurricane season.
The remnants of Kenneth produced one of the highest rainfall totals in Hawaii, reaching up to 12 inches (305 mm) on Oahu.
Document 3: Tropical Storm Katrina was a short-lived, weak tropical cyclone that produced minor damage across areas previously devastated by Hurricane Mitch in 1998.
The remnants of the storm persisted until November 1, at which time it was absorbed by a cold front.
Document 4: Hurricane Allison was the first named storm and first hurricane of the 1995 Atlantic hurricane season.
It strengthened into a tropical storm early on June 3 and into a hurricane on June 4 in the Gulf of Mexico.
Document 5: Tropical Storm Georgette was a short-lived tropical storm that struck the Baja California Sur in September 2010.
Damage was minimal and no deaths were reported in the country.
Document 6: The 1928 Haiti hurricane was considered the worst tropical cyclone in Haiti since the 1886 Indianola hurricane.
The storm completely wiped out livestock and many crops, particularly coffee, cocoa, and sugar.
Document 7: Tropical Storm Abby was an exceptionally small tropical cyclone that had minor effects across Southeast Texas in early August 1964.
Only two structures sustained damage in Matagorda, one being destroyed by a possible tornado.
